visual_description: Two men in white shirts and ties sit side by side in an office, one with arms crossed, both looking toward the right. A monitor in front of them displays a surveillance image of a person walking down a hallway. A woman in a light-colored uniform stands behind them, looking toward the right with her hands clasped.
audio_description: The audio clip begins with a sharp, high-frequency click, likely from a recording device being activated, immediately followed by a faint electronic hiss that persists throughout. The setting is a large, reverberant indoor space, such as a police station or government office, with hard surfaces and minimal sound absorption. The first male speaker, positioned close to the microphone, delivers a forceful, accusatory question in Mandarin: “你们CIB为什么跟踪黄警司？” (“Why is your CIB following Superintendent Wong?”). His tone is urgent and confrontational, with a slightly higher pitch and a clipped, assertive delivery, suggesting he is a senior officer or someone in a position of authority. A brief pause follows, during which the ambient hiss and room reverberation become more noticeable. The second male speaker, slightly more distant and with a lower, more measured tone, responds: “这是内务部的事。” (“This is an Internal Affairs matter.”). His speech is calm and authoritative, indicating a formal, procedural stance and a clear assertion of jurisdiction. The exchange is marked by a adversarial atmosphere, with both speakers maintaining a professional demeanor despite the underlying conflict. After the second speaker finishes, a soft rustling sound is heard, likely from clothing
